title: supera auditor receipt
description: The structured verdict an auditor agent (supera-security-auditor / supera-freshness-auditor) emits as its final message. Consumed by /audit, which renders the PR body and the headless signal deterministically instead of scraping the prose two-list report. One receipt per auditor; /audit merges the two.

status.description: Machine-readable verdict /audit reads first. 'ok' = ran clean, every safe fix applied (or nothing found), nothing needs a human. 'needs-review' = advisory findings (recommend/hold/flag) a human should see; non-blocking, the PR still opens. 'blocked' = could not audit at all (no lockfile, manager/probe missing) — the run is incomplete.

applied.description: Safe fixes auto-applied this run. Empty when none. security leaves its edits uncommitted (so omits `commit` — /audit makes the single commit); freshness commits each as its own atomic per-package commit and fills `commit`.

verdict.description: The applied remediation. security: upgrade (in-range) / pin (scoped override) / remove-pin (stale override). freshness: upgrade only.

target.description: The dependency acted on, e.g. 'lodash' or 'serde@1.0.197'.

from.description: Version or range before the change.

to.description: Version or range after the change.

commit.description: The atomic commit SHA carrying this single change. Filled by freshness; omitted by security (its edits ride /audit's single CVE-override commit).

verifiedBy.description: Which verification check confirmed the change green, e.g. 'test' or 'build'.

findings.description: Issues NOT auto-applied — each needs a human call. Empty when none.

verdict.description: recommend = a safe but out-of-level bump to consider. hold = latest-in-range is known-bad (yanked / regressed / inside cooldown). flag = needs a human decision (major, unverifiable, coupled set, or an unfixable CVE).

file.description: Repo-relative file the finding sits in, when locatable.

verification.description: The install/build/test run that confirmed the applied set is green (the §gate run mirroring CI). Omit a check the stack has no command for.

degraded.description: Honesty lines for degraded probes (missing cargo-audit, network failure, unverifiable publish date). Each blocked an auto-apply but never fails the run. Empty when none.

output.description: Key tail of the command output (e.g. 'N passed').
